full_text:
A) Dissociation

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase, followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H) by Maria and Foteini.

Dispase dissociation media included 18.4 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 400 µl Dispase + 200 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C rotating for 30 min, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 17,8 ml DMEM/F12+++, 2 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 200 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C, rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

Dispase dissociated cells:

(1) First RBC lysis with 3 ml for 5 min + 12 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was resuspended in 20 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media.

Sequentially dissociated cells: (1) First RBC lysis with 3 ml for 5 min + 12 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was resuspended in 20 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media.

Cell counts:

Cells plated

A total of 18 conditions were included (Datopotamab deruxtecan and its ADC control (human IgG1-deruxtecan, DAR4, four concentrations each) and Sacituzumab tirumotecan and its ADC control (human IgG1–sulfo-SPDB-DM4, five concentrations each), accounting for 40 wells with 9 drops per well. A total of 15E6 live cells were calculated for plating.

B) Biobanking

Dispase dissociated cells: 7.5E6 cells/vial total 6 vials

Cells were frozen in 1mL of STEM-CellBanker.

C) Experiment

Fresh cells were used for an ADC titration experiment using Datopotamab deruxtecan and its ADC control (human IgG1-deruxtecan, DAR4, four concentrations each) and Sacituzumab tirumotecan and its ADC control (human IgG1–sulfo-SPDB-DM4, five concentrations each). The aim was to determine (sub)optimal ADC concentrations that elicit measurable biological effects without inducing excessive cell death, followed by flow cytometry analysis.→See 15_Log_file_wet_lab on Google drive
